The following assets are publicly exposed in UEFN and modifying them will break all user-created projects using these assets. If you are:
  1. Trying to delete a released asset - Leave it
  2. Making API-incompatible changes - Create a new asset and leave the original as-is
  3. Trying to move/rename a released asset - Unexpose asset from previous release (ask in channel for confirmation and steps), make a backup of the original, move it, fix up redirectors, then restore the original
  4. Something else? Ask in #fn-devhelp-ext.

SpatialMetrics.MemoryUsage_Short: Amount of executable memory in use.
SpatialMetrics.ActorCount_Desc:
Tracks actors in the world, accounting for streaming events.
Counts actors currently loaded in the world, affected by dynamic spawning and streaming (if World Partition is enabled).
High actor counts may impact game update time, render time, memory usage, and network bandwidth.
SpatialMetrics.SceneGraphEntityCount_Desc:
Tracks entities in the world, accounting for streaming events.
Counts entities currently loaded in the world, affected by dynamic spawning and streaming (if World Partition is enabled).
Entities are part of Scene Graph, Unreal Engine's Entity-Component system.
High entity counts may impact game update time, render time, memory usage, and network bandwidth.
SpatialMetrics.TextureStreamingMemoryUsage_Desc:
Tracks memory consumed by streamed textures.
Texture streaming automatically loads and unloads texture detail based on distance and screen size.
High usage may indicate too many high-resolution textures visible simultaneously, or textures with unnecessarily large maximum mip levels.
Consider reducing texture resolutions, adjusting streaming settings per texture, or using texture atlases to share memory more efficiently.
SpatialMetrics.GpuTime_Desc:
Tracks the GPU time to render a single frame.
High values indicate GPU-bound rendering, often from complex materials, high-resolution textures, dense geometry, or excessive overdraw.
Consider simplifying materials, reducing texture resolution, using LODs, or optimising post-process effects.
SpatialMetrics.RhiTime_Desc:
Tracks the Render Hardware Interface thread time.
High values often correlate with high draw call counts or complex rendering workloads.
Consider optimising draw calls and render complexity if this metric is elevated.
SpatialMetrics.GameUpdateTime_Desc:
Tracks the game thread time to update a single frame.
Measures time spent on game logic, including actor updates, collision detection, and gameplay code.
High values indicate CPU-bound game logic.
SpatialMetrics.DrawCallCount_Desc:
Tracks the number of draw calls issued in a single frame.
Each draw call has CPU overhead.
Minimise draw calls through instancing, batching, or merging meshes where appropriate.
SpatialMetrics.PrimitiveCount_Desc:
Tracks the number of primitives rendered in a single frame.
Primitives are individual mesh components, including static meshes, skeletal meshes, and particle systems.
High counts may impact GPU performance and memory bandwidth.
Consider using LODs to reduce mesh complexity at distance, culling off-screen objects, or merging static meshes to reduce primitive counts.
SpatialMetrics.ShadersLoadedCount_Desc:
Tracks the number of shader variations currently loaded in memory.
Each unique material configuration generates shader variations.
High counts may indicate too many unique materials or complex material settings.
Consider consolidating materials or simplifying material parameters.
SpatialMetrics.UniqueCollisionMeshCount_Desc:
Tracks the number of unique collision meshes loaded in memory.
Each unique collision mesh consumes memory and affects physics simulation performance.
Consider using simpler collision primitives or shared collision assets where possible.
SpatialMetrics.AvailableMemory_Desc:
Tracks the platform's available physical memory.
Use this to evaluate headroom for each target platform.
Lower values indicate memory pressure and potential performance degradation.
SpatialMetrics.MemoryUsage_Desc:
Tracks the platform's executable memory usage.
Use this to evaluate memory requirements for each target platform.
Higher values indicate increased memory consumption and potential performance degradation.
SpatialMetrics.RenderTime_Desc:
Tracks the render thread time to update a single frame.
Measures time spent preparing draw commands and managing rendering resources.
High values may indicate render thread bottlenecks.
SpatialMetrics.UObjectCount_Desc:
Tracks the total number of UObject instances in memory.
UObjects include actors, components, assets, and most engine objects.
High counts may indicate excessive spawned objects, loaded assets, or opportunities to use object pooling.
Consider despawning unused actors, unloading unnecessary assets, or implementing pooling for frequently spawned objects.
SpatialMetrics.FrameTime_Desc:
Tracks the total time to update a single frame.
Includes both Game Update Time and Render Time.
This represents the actual frame duration experienced by users.
